message:
refactor(cass,caut): replace env::set_var test hooks with typed override slots

Rust 2024 marks `std::env::set_var` as unsafe (process-global, non-thread-
safe), and this workspace forbids its use. The mcp_conformance_additional_tools
harness still mutated $PATH via `unsafe { set_var }` to steer cass/caut
invocations to fake binaries — which would panic/UB under concurrent
tests and blocks any future clippy/lint pass that bans the unsafe env API.

Production change (cass.rs, caut.rs)
  - Add a process-wide `OnceLock<Mutex<Option<String>>>` override slot
    per binary
  - New `set_cass_cli_override(Option<String>)` / `set_caut_cli_override`
    public fns (doc comment spells out the Rust 2024 rationale)
  - `CassClient::default()` / `CautClient::default()` now read
    `cass_binary()` / `caut_binary()` which consults the slot before
    falling back to the bare PATH lookup ("cass" / "caut")
  - No change to the regular-user path: if no override is set, behavior
    is identical to prior $PATH resolution

Test harness (mcp_conformance_additional_tools.rs)
  - Drop `TestEnvGuard { old_path }` + the `unsafe { set_var }` /
    `remove_var` block; replace with a zero-field `TestToolOverrideGuard`
    whose Drop simply clears the three tool overrides (wezterm, cass, caut)
  - Rename `env_lock` → `tool_override_lock` to reflect what it now guards
  - Write the fake binaries into a tempdir as before, then register
    their paths via `set_cass_cli_override` / `set_caut_cli_override`
    instead of prepending the dir to $PATH

Net effect: zero unsafe in the test harness, no leaked $PATH mutation
across tests, and the production side gains a first-class test seam
matching the `set_wezterm_cli_override` pattern already in use.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
